card.intuition: Special functions that can be paused and resumed, allowing them to produce a sequence of values over time instead of all at once.
card.analogy: A vending machine: you press a button (call `.next()`), and one snack (value) pops out. Then the machine waits patiently until the next person comes by to press the button again. It doesn't drop all 100 snacks at once.
when_to_use: Handling infinitely large data streams (e.g., Fibonacci numbers)
Implementing custom async flow control (used extensively by the Redux-Saga library)
gotchas: Generators cannot be 'restarted'; once the `done` property is true, calling `.next()` will just keep returning `undefined`
You can send values BACK into the generator by passing an argument to `.next(val)`

exercise.prompt: Complete the generator function in Python (same logic as JS).
hints: You 'give up' or 'produce' a value with this keyword
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "yield"
assert check("yield")
print("PASS")
